text.title: AI-Generated Dockerfile Red-Flag List Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments of ai-dockerfile-redflag-list. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Agent leaves `FROM node:latest` and base policy never catches it because the policy file is unread.
antipattern.root-cause: Red-flag list is documented but never wired to CI; review is voluntary.
antipattern.fix: Wire `validate-ai-dockerfile-redflag-list.py` to PR check; block merge on `verdict=fail`.

antipattern.symptom: Build pushes image with `ARG NPM_TOKEN` baked into a layer; `docker history` leaks the token.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent ported a CI workflow that used build args for secrets; never switched to BuildKit secrets.

antipattern.symptom: `HEALTHCHECK CMD true` ships; orchestrator reports green forever.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent added healthcheck to satisfy the rule mechanically without a real probe.
antipattern.fix: Require `HEALTHCHECK` payload to call an app endpoint (e.g. `curl -fsS http://localhost:8080/healthz`) and validate non-trivial body.
